So this is not a measurement protocol.

Uh, could be applicable to uh, in-situ O&M direct expert or alternate marketing method as, uh, these produce, uh, information that needs to be, uh, collected and correlated.

So hybrid uh, two step by the nature, it's, uh, improves the correlation process as it collects, uh, information being collected over, uh, sequence of, uh, trigger packet, whether it's a just one trigger packet or a sequence, uh, then it could be statistically processed locally and then collected, correlated.

And um, for the network analytics, it could be used in point to point, point to multipoint modes without unnecessary replication of their collected information Because, uh, it, uh, creates a separate, uh, follow up package on each branch if it's a point to multipoint three downstream.
And as it can collect information in downstream direction, it can collect information in upstream direction direction.
So it removes the limit of amount of information because, for example, one of the issues that we know about from, uh, trace option in in situ O&M as it requires the space in a data packet itself.
So there are amount of information and number of transit nodes can be, uh, concern and limit the amount of information being collected.
So some nodes would downstream further downstream might not be reporting information.
There are hybrid two step operates by adding new packets to the train of collected information, so thus there is no physical limit.
At the same time, the advantage of hybrid two step could be seen as it separates the generation of information and collection and transport information.

So which improves the accuracy, for example, of delay measurement, because it's a challenging for, uh, taking timestamp on transit and putting it in the same packet that's supposed to transport it and adjusting the checksum because it's in the header.
Comparing to, uh, FCS code in Ethernet.
So, uh, by separating these two events of taking timestamp and transporting it, it could be taken when they're, uh, physical process of transmitting the first byte begins.
So thus making the timestamp consistent in avoiding unnecessary variation because of the queuing delay.
Um, so, uh, at the same time.
